title: Wikipedia: The Missing Manual
excerpt: A comprehensive practical guide to editing and contributing to Wikipedia, covering everything from basic editing to advanced community norms, dispute resolution, and content creation.
keyIdeas: Wikipedia is not just a reference to consume but a community to participate in
The Wikipedia community has developed complex norms, policies, and dispute resolution mechanisms
Effective Wikipedia editing requires understanding both the technical markup and the social dynamics
keyTakeaways: Anyone can edit Wikipedia, but effective editing requires understanding community norms
Wikipedia's five pillars form the foundation of all editorial policy
Dispute resolution is a structured process with multiple stages
Reliable sources are the backbone of Wikipedia content
Wikipedia's quality has improved dramatically since its early years
whoShouldRead: New Wikipedia editors wanting to contribute effectively
Educators teaching information literacy and digital citizenship
Experienced editors wanting to deepen their understanding of policies
Researchers studying online collaboration and knowledge production
whoShouldSkip: Readers who only want to consume Wikipedia content, not create it
Those seeking a history of Wikipedia rather than a practical guide
Users who prefer video tutorials to written manuals
